title: LOLBins (living-off-the-land binaries)

headings.text: Rundll32 Abuse for Indirect Command Execution

overview: LOLBins (Living Off the Land Binaries) are legitimate, pre-installed executables and scripts—integral parts of the operating system or common software packages—that have normal administrative functions. Their inherent trust (often due to digital signing by the vendor) makes them ideal for attackers who wish to avoid introducing external malicious code. Key c

study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
